Title: Single-molecule real-time sequencing reveals diverse allelic variations in carotenoid biosynthetic genes in pepper (Capsicum spp.)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 94 accessions; 43 accessions used for UPLC carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the first committed step of carotenoid biosynthesis by condensing geranylgeranyl diphosphate molecules to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: open-field grown plants; mature fruit stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional PSY1 is required for high carotenoid accumulation and, together with functional CCS, for red fruit; PSY1 knockout reduces total carotenoids and shifts fruit color to deep orange/orange/pink or ivory/light yellow depending on CCS status.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted SMRT sequencing of alleles; PCR analysis of structural variants; UPLC carotenoid profiling correlated with genotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple knockout alleles of PSY1 were identified, including frameshift, nonsense, and large insertion variants. 2. PSY1 genotype strongly correlated with carotenoid level classes across accessions. 3. Accessions with dysfunctional PSY1 still accumulated basal carotenoids, suggesting partial compensation by other PSY homologs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two structural insertion alleles were characterized: psy1-in1 (~1395 bp insertion in intron 3) in ACN64 likely does not abolish function, whereas psy1-in2 (~7 kb insertion) in ACN69 is associated with impaired function. Eighteen additional accessions lacked amplification and may contain major structural variants. One key gene highlighted by authors as major determinant of fruit color.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PSY1 loss lowers total carotenoid content and prevents full red coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted SMRT sequencing; UPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for functional markers predicting pepper fruit color and for metabolic engineering or breeding for carotenoid content/color classes.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 94 accessions; 43 accessions used for UPLC carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes terminal conversion in the pepper-specific branch leading to red keto-carotenoids such as capsanthin/capsorubin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for red pigment accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: open-field grown plants; mature fruit stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional CCS is required for capsanthin accumulation and red fruit; CCS knockout or large promoter/coding-region structural mutations abolish or strongly reduce capsanthin and produce yellow/orange fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted SMRT sequencing; promoter/coding-region PCR for structural variation; UPLC carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCS carried the highest number of knockout-type alleles, including frameshift, nonsense, promoter deletions, and a large coding-region insertion. 2. CCS dysfunction was strongly associated with absence of capsanthin and non-red fruit. 3. Structural promoter alleles ccs-p1 and ccs-p2 abolished capsanthin accumulation, while ccs-p3 may allow minimal residual expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Major alleles include ccs-3, ccs-c3, ccs-c11, ccs-p1, ccs-p2, ccs-p3, and ccs-in. ccs-p1 is a 4879 bp deletion spanning promoter to coding region; ccs-p2 is a 2968 bp promoter deletion; ccs-in is ~3 kb insertion in coding region. ACN38 accumulated capsanthin despite nonsense allele ccs-c9, suggesting possible additional CCS-like function. Identified by authors as one of two principal genes explaining fruit color variation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CCS loss prevents red pigment accumulation and shifts carotenoid composition toward lutein/antheraxanthin/violaxanthin-rich non-red phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted SMRT sequencing; UPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoters; natural promoter structural variants ccs-p1, ccs-p2, ccs-p3 identified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly valuable marker for breeding red versus non-red peppers and for engineering capsanthin accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putative phytoene synthase catalyzing phytoene formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: open-field grown plants; mature fruit stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PSY2 showed only missense variants and no clear knockout alleles; authors infer it may support basal carotenoid synthesis in PSY1-knockout accessions, but direct causal proof is lacking.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted SMRT sequencing; comparative genotype-metabolite analysis/inference
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY2 sequence was highly conserved across Capsicum accessions. 2. Only missense variants were detected, with no evident loss-of-function alleles. 3. Authors propose intact PSY2 may explain residual carotenoid synthesis in PSY1-knockout accessions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as possible compensatory homolog rather than decisive determinant of fruit color in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for future functional studies on compensatory carotenoid biosynthesis, but limited immediate marker utility from this study.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate toward downstream xanthophylls and capsanthin branch
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: open-field grown plants; mature fruit stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation was detected, including one frameshift allele and amplification failures, but these did not strongly correlate with fruit color in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted SMRT sequencing; PCR amplification pattern analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seven coding-region variants were detected in Lcyb. 2. A frameshift allele (lcyb-c1) occurred in two C. pubescens accessions. 3. Structural/amplification abnormalities in four C. annuum accessions were not significantly correlated with fruit color.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors concluded Lcyb was not a major explanatory gene for color variation here due to limited knockout variation and weak genotype-phenotype correlation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as supplementary carotenoid-pathway marker, but not primary predictor of fruit color in this dataset.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: open-field grown plants; mature fruit stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coding and structural variants were found, but no decisive association with fruit color classes was established in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted SMRT sequencing; PCR analysis of structural variants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nine amino-acid-changing coding variants were identified in CrtZ-2. 2. A 960 bp intronic insertion and an 84 bp deletion were detected in some accessions. 3. Accessions with CrtZ-2 structural variation displayed wide fruit-color ranges, indicating weak explanatory power for color phenotype here.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: All 17 accessions with the 960 bp insertion were C. annuum and ranged from light yellow to red fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful in future fine-mapping of xanthophyll composition, but not a primary determinant in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: epoxidizes zeaxanthin to antheraxanthin and violaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: open-field grown plants; mature fruit stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZEP showed many missense alleles but no reported knockout mutations, and it did not emerge as a major determinant of fruit-color variation in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sixteen amino-acid-changing ZEP variants were identified. 2. Several variants were species-specific, especially in C. pubescens. 3. No clear causal relationship with major color groups was established.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: All accessions amplified normally for ZEP; no major structural defects reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for carotenoid composition studies, but limited direct predictive value for mature fruit color in this paper.
